definitions: The geometry of stereo vision, specifically the geometric relationship between two camera views of the same 3D scene.
A fundamental concept in computer vision and photogrammetry that describes the constraints on the location of corresponding points in two images of a 3D scene.
examples: Understanding Epipolar Geometry is crucial for 3D reconstruction.
The algorithm relies heavily on Epipolar Geometry to match features.
Researchers are exploring new applications of Epipolar Geometry.
